documentation.value:
Prints one or more expressions to standard output, separated by OFS, terminated by ORS.
```awk
print $1, $2
print "Total:", sum
```
One or more expressions to print, separated by commas (OFS).

Splits `string` into pieces separated by `separator` and stores in `array`. Returns the number of pieces.
```awk
n = split("a:b:c", arr, ":")
# arr[1]="a", arr[2]="b", arr[3]="c", n=3
```
The input string to split.
The array to store the resulting pieces.
The field separator regex or string. Defaults to FS if omitted.

Substitutes the first occurrence of `regexp` in `target` with `replacement`. Returns 1 if substitution made, 0 otherwise.
```awk
sub(/old/, "new", $0)
```
The regular expression pattern to match.
The replacement string. Use `&` to reference the matched text.
The target string variable. Defaults to `$0` if omitted.

Globally substitutes all occurrences of `regexp` in `target` with `replacement`. Returns the number of substitutions.
```awk
gsub(/[[:space:]]/, "_", $0)
```
The regular expression pattern to match.
The replacement string. Use `&` to reference the matched text.
The target string variable. Defaults to `$0` if omitted.

Returns the length of string `s`, or the number of elements in an array.
```awk
len = length("hello")  # 5
len = length(arr)       # number of elements
```
A string or array. If omitted, uses `$0`.

Returns a copy of `string` with all uppercase characters converted to lowercase.
```awk
print tolower("HELLO")  # "hello"
```
The input string to convert.

Returns a copy of `string` with all lowercase characters converted to uppercase.
```awk
print toupper("hello")  # "HELLO"
```
The input string to convert.

Returns the position of the first occurrence of `target` in `string`, or 0 if not found.
```awk
pos = index("hello world", "world")  # 7
```
The string to search in.
The substring to search for.

Searches `string` for the longest leftmost match of `regexp`. Sets RSTART and RLENGTH. Returns RSTART or 0.
```awk
if (match($0, /[0-9]+/)) print substr($0, RSTART, RLENGTH)
```
The string to search in.
The regular expression pattern to match.

Returns a string formatted according to `format` (printf-style), without printing.
```awk
s = sprintf("%05d", 42)       # "00042"
s = sprintf("%-10s %d", name, age)
```
A printf-style format string (`%d`, `%s`, `%f`, etc.).
Values to substitute into the format string.

Returns a substring of `string` starting at position `start` (1-based) with optional `length`.
```awk
s = substr("hello", 2, 3)  # "ell"
s = substr("hello", 2)     # "ello"
```
The source string.
Starting position (1-based).
Optional number of characters to extract. If omitted, returns to end of string.

Executes `command` as a shell command and returns its exit status.
```awk
system("ls -la")
ret = system("mkdir -p output")
```
The shell command string to execute.
